title: Energy manufacturing method from industrial and household waste and a suitable reactor
abstract: The present invention relates to a method of producing energy from industrial and household (domestic) waste used in the production of any kind of organic-based industrial and urban waste by complying with environmental conditions and generating energy, and a reactor according to this method, a first pipe line (8) for allowing the wastes to be transported upwards through the waste inlet (3), a transfer pipe (4) for transferring the wastes to the uppermost point in the first pipe line (2), to which the wastes are transferred in the non gasified waste transfer pipe (7), the second pipeline (6) to which the non gasified waste transfer pipe (7) is transferred to the gasification unit (5) the ash outlet (8) located in the lower part of the gasifier unit (5) constitutes the unit chasis (10) of the gasifier unit (5) which retains the filter syngas output (9) and the gasifier unit (5) located in the upper lower part of the gasifier unit (5).
claims:
Claims 1. The invention is about a reactor for producing energy from industrial and household (domestic) waste, and it is characterized in that; - a waste entry (3) where the waste enters the system first, - a first pipeline (2) providing uptake of waste entering the waste inlet (3), - A transfer pipe (4) through which the waste to the uppermost point in the first pipeline (2) is transferred, - the gasifier unit (5) through the transfer pipe (4) to which the wastes have entered, - non gasified waste transfer pipe (7) for gaseous effluent discharged from the gasifier unit (5) - the second pipeline (6) to which wastes are transferred in the non gasified waste transfer pipe (7), - ash outlet (8) at the bottom of the second pipeline (6) - the filter syngas outlet (9) in the upper underside of the gasifier unit (5) and - unit chasis (10) which maintains the gasifier unit (5). A reactor for producing energy from industrial and household (domestic) waste as claimed in claim 1, characterized in that by having a gasifier unit (5) with unit input (5.1) and unit output (5.2) on it. A reactor for energy production from industrial and domestic waste as claimed in claim 1, characterized in that, the waste from the transfer pipe (4) has a gasifier unit (5) containing the unit entrance (5.1) of the first entry. 4. A reactor for producing energy from industrial and household (domestic) waste as claimed in claim 1, characterized in that, the gassing unit (5) has non gasified waste transfer tubing (7) to which non gasfied waste is transferred from the unit outlet (5.2). 5. A reactor for producing energy from industrial and household (domestic) waste as claimed in claim 1, characterized in that, it has a first pipe line (2) carrying the wastes upwards by the rotary rail system located on the inside. 6. A reactor for producing energy from industrial and household (domestic) waste as claimed in claim 1, characterized in that, it has a second pipeline (6) carrying the wastes up by the rotary rail system located on the inside and transferring the wastes to the transfer pipe (4). 7. The production of a reactor which provides energy production from industrial and domestic waste as claimed in claim 1, characterized in that, the gasifier has an ash outlet (8) which allows the resulting ash to be removed from the system. 8. A reactor for producing energy from industrial and household (domestic) waste as claimed in claim 1, characterized in that by having a gasifier unit (5) which gasifies the wastes within 1200Â°C. 9. A reactor for generating energy from industrial and household (domestic) waste as claimed in claim 1, characterized in that, by the presence of an automatic control system that maintains the temperature of the gasifier unit (5) between 1000Â°C and 1200Â°C or provides heat control. 10. A reactor for producing energy from industrial and household (domestic) waste as claimed in claim 1, characterized in that, by having a gasifier unit (5) with a coating resistant to 3500Â°C degrees on the inside. 11. A reactor for generating energy from industrial and household (domestic) waste as claimed in claim 1, characterized in that, it has a carrier frame (1) which carries all the parts in the system on the floor. 12. The method of producing energy from industrial and household waste in a reactor as claimed in claim 1, - the introduction of overheated air into the oxidation reaction with hydrocarbons given by the equation 02 + C = C02 + 1 Â° 02 + 2 H2 = 2 H20 + t Â°, 

 - entry of products entering the oxidation reaction into the screw type gasifier unit (5) - overheated gasifier unit (5) (up to 1200 Â° C), oxidation reaction products (C02 gas + H20 vapor) in the C + C02 = 2C0 C + 2 H20 = C02 + 2 H2 reduction with hydrocarbons raw material Reaction and - the conversion of C02, H20, H2, Co, N2 mixture gases into synthetic gas by a filter is the end result of the products entering the reduction reaction. - the conversion of C02, H20, H2, Co, N2 mixture gases into synthetic gas by a filter is the end result of the products entering the reduction reaction. 13. The method of producing energy from industrial and household (domestic) waste in a reactor as claimed in claim 12, characterized in that the formed synthetic gas is separated from the screw type gasifier unit (5) and sent to the separator (the mechanical wastes and the scrubber where the residues are cleaned) to form H20 and CmHn.
